stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML topics like transformers and text-generation.
The presence of Llama indicates relevance to the competitive landscape of large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core areas of NLP/AI, specifically text-generation using transformer architectures.
The model is associated with Meta's Llama family, indicating relevance in the competitive LLM landscape.

stories.title: NASA selects Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for a 2028 mission to Mars
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: NASA has selected Relativity Space, a rocket company, for a 2028 mission to Mars.
The partnership involves Relativity Space providing the spacecraft, rocket, and cruise operations for the Aeolus payload.
The selection represents a significant public-private partnership in deep space exploration.

stories.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft has identified a new, lightweight backdoor malware dubbed 'Crypto Clipper'.
The malware is capable of stealing cryptocurrency from infected devices.
It propagates via USB drives and utilizes the Tor network for communication.

stories.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
stories.author: Abdul Rafay Syed
bullet_points: Researchers found a 'causally actionable activation-space direction' that correlates with emergent misalignment in language models.
This direction successfully separates aligned and misaligned activations with 99.6% accuracy across four different model families (Qwen2.5, Gemma-2, Llama-3.2, Mistral-3).
The study addresses the risk of fine-tuning models on insecure code leading to poorly understood internal structural misalignment.

stories.title: Hyundai buys Boston Dynamics
stories.author: ck2
bullet_points: Hyundai has acquired Boston Dynamics, a major robotics company.
This acquisition signals a significant strategic move into advanced robotics and automation.
The deal represents a major industry shift in the application of sophisticated mobile robotics.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is text-generation, indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.
The tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements translate to higher quality model outputs and faster response times.
The updated engine also demonstrates better memory efficiency when running models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon devices for optimized performance.
The update is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no manual environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: FDA advisors unanimously vote to approve Moderna's mRNA after agency drama
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: The FDA advisors unanimously approved Moderna's mRNA vaccine.
The approval followed a period of 'agency drama,' notably involving a Trump official refusing review.
This marks a significant regulatory milestone for mRNA vaccine technology.

stories.title: As China looms, Taiwan makes more drones for defense and the US military
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Taiwan is increasing its drone production capabilities for national defense.
The increased defense spending in Taiwan is expected to have positive economic spillover effects overseas.
The development is framed within the geopolitical context of potential threats from China.

stories.title: Android verification is coming: Google confirms timeline and supported app stores
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is implementing a new Android verification system service this month.
Significant changes related to this verification system are scheduled to begin in September.
The update confirms timelines and details regarding supported app stores for the new system.

stories.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
stories.author: Nityanand Mathur
bullet_points: FlowEdit addresses a key limitation in state-of-the-art flow-matching TTS: the inability to adapt to new pronunciations post-deployment.
It introduces a lifelong adaptation framework that corrects pronunciation errors by applying latent conditioning edits rather than retraining the entire model.
The method optimizes token-level perturbations in the text embedding space when corrective feedback is provided, enabling efficient, targeted updates.

stories.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
stories.author: Jana Diesner
bullet_points: Introduces StylisticBias, a new controlled benchmark for evaluating attribute-level social bias in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a critical gap in MLLM research by isolating the impact of visual cues (appearance) from inherent identity differences when models judge people.
Highlights the growing societal risk associated with MLLMs deployed in consequential settings due to poorly understood visual bias.

stories.title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
stories.author: Hamed Zamani
bullet_points: The research proposes a new method for LLM alignment that moves beyond expensive explicit human feedback.
It leverages implicit user feedback (like mouse movements and eye tracking) to gauge user preference.
This approach addresses the limitations of current methods by tapping into readily available, vital internet behavioral data.

stories.title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
stories.author: Thomas Brox
bullet_points: Introduces RefRad2D, a large-scale bilingual (German/English) dataset (1.2M pairs) for radiology image-text tasks.
Presents RefRad2D, which leverages LLM-based curation and automated segmentation to reduce the need for manual spatial annotations.
Develops RadGrounder, a model capable of jointly performing report generation, visual question answering, and spatial grounding in radiology.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation capabilities on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machine.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Hue’s wired wall modules bring non-smart lights into its ecosystem
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Philips Hue introduced wired wall modules to integrate non-smart lights into its ecosystem.
The new modules are designed to be installed behind existing wall switches.
Hue also launched more affordable versions of its Signe series lamps and upgraded E14 bulbs.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Sudarshan Kamath
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of clarity regarding how individual words influence acoustic output in style-captioned Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
It introduces a novel cross-attention attribution method, adapting the DAAM framework to the speech domain for the first time.
The method generates per-token heatmaps across multiple layers of speech diffusion models to enhance controllability and diagnose TTS failure modes.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to manage structured task states for AI agents.
Addresses a key limitation in current agents where state (facts, constraints) must be reconstructed from the prompt each turn.
Focuses on improving policy-adherence and state management, particularly relevant for complex customer-service AI applications.

stories.title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
stories.author: Fengxiang He
bullet_points: Introduces DeepSWIP, a new framework to enable counterfactual reasoning in neurosymbolic systems like DeepProbLog.
Addresses the limitation of standard associational inference by providing a single-world counterfactual semantics.
Achieves this by using neural materialization to translate neural predicates into standard ProbLog choices and applying SWIPs.

stories.title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
stories.author: Georgia Channing
bullet_points: Introduces SARLO-80, a new large-scale dataset designed to bridge the gap between optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) data.
Addresses a limitation in current multimodal AI training by providing high-resolution SAR data that preserves complex-valued measurements and native acquisition geometry.
The development is crucial for advancing physically grounded multimodal foundation models in remote sensing applications.

stories.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
stories.author: Dmitrii Babaev
bullet_points: The paper introduces Multi-LCB, an extension of the LiveCodeBench (LCB) benchmark.
LCB is a recognized tool for evaluating LLMs on code generation, currently limited to Python.
Multi-LCB aims to address this limitation by extending the benchmark to support multiple programming languages, testing generalization.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and another unspecified component.

stories.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
stories.author: Karthik Sridharan
bullet_points: Introduces 'privacy via predictability' as a fine-grained alternative to traditional Differential Privacy (DP).
Addresses the costly worst-case privacy-accuracy tradeoff inherent in standard DP.
The framework explicitly models attacker knowledge, compromised data portions, and query families to measure leakage.

stories.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
stories.author: Liang Su
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in LLM serving by focusing on low-latency, small-batch, on-device scenarios, unlike traditional high-throughput systems.
Proposes 'Execution-State Capsules' to manage the full execution state, not just the KV cache, which is crucial for interactive agents.
Targets use cases like interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies that require frequent branching and interruption.

stories.title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
stories.author: Jeannie Albrecht
bullet_points: The research addresses the need for LLM coding agents to possess higher-level, operational knowledge about a software repository beyond just the code itself.
It investigates the effectiveness of using supplementary context files (like AGENTS.md) to guide these coding agents.
The paper proposes a 'Probe-and-Refine Tuning' method to improve agent performance, addressing conflicting prior findings on guidance utility.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to give policy-adherent agents structured state management.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where task state (facts, constraints, conditions) is implicitly reconstructed from the prompt.
Focuses on improving reliability in complex domains like customer service by explicitly tracking state across multi-turn interactions and tool calls.

stories.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
stories.author: Marco Guerini
bullet_points: Addresses the intersection of hate speech and misinformation, a gap in current NLP research.
Proposes a new dataset, 'CATCH-ME if you RAG,' to provide high-quality examples for generating counterspeech.
Aims to improve LLM performance in generating nuanced and non-repetitive responses against harmful online exchanges.

stories.title: Token-Operations-Oriented Inference Optimization Techniques for Large Models
stories.author: Qinghuai Ma
bullet_points: The paper focuses on token-oriented inference optimization, a critical area for making large models scalable and cost-effective.
It introduces a novel four-layer technical architecture: Multi-model Fusion, Model Optimization, Compute-Model Fusion, and Compute-Network-Model Fusion.
The research provides a systematic review of current key technologies and industry status across these optimization levels.

stories.title: PsyScore: A Psychometrically-Aware Framework for Trait-Adaptive Essay Scoring and ZPD-Scaffolded Feedback
stories.author: Chanjin Zheng
bullet_points: Introduces PsyScore, a framework designed to improve Automated Essay Scoring (AES) by integrating psychological principles.
Addresses the limitation of current systems where scoring and feedback are treated separately, often lacking instructional relevance.
The framework aims to provide psychometrically-aware, trait-adaptive scoring and scaffolding feedback, moving beyond simple LLM outputs.

stories.title: The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
stories.author: Celestine Achi
bullet_points: Introduces the Meaning Intelligence Framework (MIF) to analyze Nigerian public discourse.
MIF is a nine-dimension schema designed to differentiate true communicative intent from surface sentiment.
Argues that AI failure in Nigerian discourse stems from context failure, not just translation issues, challenging existing sentiment benchmarks.

stories.title: I used sound waves to make espresso. It could cut coffee‑brewing energy use by ¾
stories.author: zeristor
bullet_points: A novel method utilizes sound waves to facilitate espresso brewing.
The technology claims a potential energy reduction of up to three-quarters in the brewing process.
The concept presents a niche, but potentially impactful, application of physics/acoustics to consumer appliances.

stories.title: Project Valhalla, Explained: How a Decade of Work Arrives in JDK 28
stories.author: jvm-weekly.com via Yogthos
bullet_points: The article discusses Project Valhalla, a significant, decade-long effort within the Java Development Kit (JDK).
Project Valhalla's arrival in JDK 28 represents a major evolution in Java's capabilities.
The content is highly relevant to developers and the Java ecosystem, focusing on a core language feature update.
